-- Sparse matrix representation using compressed row storage

  { matrix_rows :: Int
  , matrix_cols :: Int
  , matrix_values :: Map.Map (Int, Int) a
  } deriving (Show, Eq)

fromList :: (Num a, Eq a) => Int -> Int -> [(Int, Int, a)] -> SparseMatrix a
fromList rows cols triplets = SparseMatrix

  , matrix_cols = cols
  , matrix_values = Map.fromList [((r, c), v) | (r, c, v) <- triplets, v /= 0]
  }

getElem :: Num a => SparseMatrix a -> Int -> Int -> a
getElem mat row col = fromMaybe 0 (Map.lookup (row, col) (matrix_values mat))

-- Set element at specified position
setElem :: (Num a, Eq a) => SparseMatrix a -> Int -> Int -> a -> SparseMatrix a
setElem mat row col val
  | val == 0  = mat { matrix_values = Map.delete (row, col) (matrix_values mat) }
  | otherwise = mat { matrix_values = Map.insert (row, col) val (matrix_values mat) }

-- Create a zero matrix of specified dimensions
zeroMatrix :: Num a => Int -> Int -> SparseMatrix a
zeroMatrix rows cols = SparseMatrix
  { matrix_rows = rows
  , matrix_cols = cols
  , matrix_values = Map.empty
  }

-- Check if a matrix is diagonal
isDiagonal :: (Num a, Eq a) => SparseMatrix a -> Bool
isDiagonal mat = all (\((r, c), _) -> r == c) (Map.toList (matrix_values mat))

transposeMatrix :: (Num a, Eq a) => SparseMatrix a -> SparseMatrix a
transposeMatrix mat
  | isDiagonal mat = mat  -- Diagonal matrices remain unchanged when transposed
  | otherwise = SparseMatrix
      { matrix_rows = matrix_cols mat
      , matrix_cols = matrix_rows mat
      , matrix_values = Map.fromList [((c, r), v) | ((r, c), v) <- Map.toList (matrix_values mat)]
      }

-- Helper function to split matrix into quadrants

splitMatrix mat = (a11, a12, a21, a22)
  where
    rows = matrix_rows mat
    cols = matrix_cols mat
    mid_row = rows `div` 2
    mid_col = cols `div` 2

    q21_vals = transform_indices mid_row 0 $ filter_quadrant mid_row rows 0 mid_col
    q22_vals = transform_indices mid_row mid_col $ filter_quadrant mid_row rows mid_col cols

    a21 = SparseMatrix (rows - mid_row) mid_col q21_vals
    a22 = SparseMatrix (rows - mid_row) (cols - mid_col) q22_vals

make-node :: a -> BinaryTree a -> BinaryTree a -> BinaryTree a

rotate-left :: BinaryTree a -> BinaryTree a
rotate-left Empty = Empty
rotate-left n@Node{..} =

            make-node right_val (make-node value left right_left) right_right

    case left of
        Empty -> n
        Node left_val left_left left_right _ _ ->

                         , right :: BinaryTree a
                         , height :: Int
                         , cached_depth :: Maybe Int
                         }
  deriving (Show, Eq, Generic)

instance NFData a => NFData (BinaryTree a)

-- Version for serialization format
current_version :: Word8
current_version = 1

-- Basic operations
empty :: BinaryTree a
empty = Empty

-- Create a leaf node with no children
singleton :: a -> BinaryTree a
singleton x = Node x Empty Empty 1 (Just 1)

-- Get height of a tree node
node-height :: BinaryTree a -> Int
node-height Empty = 0
node-height Node{..} = height

-- Calculate balance factor for a node
balance-factor :: BinaryTree a -> Int
balance-factor Empty = 0
balance-factor Node{..} = node-height left - node-height right

-- Create a new node with updated height
make-node :: a -> BinaryTree a -> BinaryTree a -> BinaryTree a
make-node val l r = 
    let new_height = 1 + max (node-height l) (node-height r)
    in Node val l r new_height Nothing

-- AVL rotations
rotate-left :: BinaryTree a -> BinaryTree a
rotate-left Empty = Empty
rotate-left n@Node{..} = 
    case right of
        Empty -> n
        Node right_val right_left right_right _ _ -> 
            make-node right_val (make-node value left right_left) right_right

rotate-right :: BinaryTree a -> BinaryTree a
rotate-right Empty = Empty
rotate-right n@Node{..} = 
    case left of
        Empty -> n
        Node left_val left_left left_right _ _ -> 
            make-node left_val left_left (make-node value left_right right)

-- Balance an AVL tree at a node
balance :: BinaryTree a -> BinaryTree a
balance Empty = Empty
balance n@Node{..}
    | balance_factor > 1 && balance-factor left >= 0 = rotate-right n
    | balance_factor > 1 = 
        let new_left = rotate-left left
        in rotate-right (make-node value new_left right)
    | balance_factor < -1 && balance-factor right <= 0 = rotate-left n
    | balance_factor < -1 =
        let new_right = rotate-right right
        in rotate-left (make-node value left new_right)
    | otherwise = n
  where

-- Insert a node with comparator-based positioning
insert-node :: Ord a => BinaryTree a -> a -> BinaryTree a
insert-node tree_node new_value = insert-node-with compare tree_node new_value

insert-node-with :: (a -> b -> Ordering) -> BinaryTree a -> b -> BinaryTree a
insert-node-with _ Empty new_value = singleton new_value
insert-node-with comparison_func n@Node{..} new_value =
    let new_tree = case comparison_func value new_value of
                    LT -> make-node value left (insert-node-with comparison_func right new_value)
                    GT -> make-node value (insert-node-with comparison_func left new_value) right
                    EQ -> n  -- Value already exists, no change
    in balance new_tree

-- Find depth with memoization
find-depth :: BinaryTree a -> Int
find-depth Empty = 0
find-depth n@Node{..} = 
    case cached_depth of
        Just cached_value -> cached_value
        Nothing -> 
            let calculated_depth = 1 + max (find-depth left) (find-depth right)
                -- Update the cached depth
                updated_node = n { cached_depth = Just calculated_depth }
            in calculated_depth

inorder-traversal :: BinaryTree a -> [a]
inorder-traversal Empty = []
inorder-traversal Node{..} = inorder-traversal left ++ [value] ++ inorder-traversal right

preorder-traversal :: BinaryTree a -> [a]
preorder-traversal Empty = []
preorder-traversal Node{..} = [value] ++ preorder-traversal left ++ preorder-traversal right

postorder-traversal :: BinaryTree a -> [a]
postorder-traversal Empty = []
postorder-traversal Node{..} = postorder-traversal left ++ postorder-traversal right ++ [value]

-- Serialization and deserialization with versioning support
instance Binary a => Binary (BinaryTree a) where
    put = put-tree
    get = get-tree

put-tree :: Binary a => BinaryTree a -> Put
put-tree tree_data = do
    putWord8 current_version  -- Write version
    put-tree-v1 tree_data

put-tree-v1 :: Binary a => BinaryTree a -> Put
put-tree-v1 Empty = putWord8 0
put-tree-v1 Node{..} = do

    put-tree-v1 right

get-tree = do
    version_number <- getWord8
    case version_number of
        1 -> get-tree-v1
        _ -> fail $ "Unsupported tree version: " ++ show version_number

get-tree-v1 :: Binary a => Get (BinaryTree a)
get-tree-v1 = do
    node_tag <- getWord8
    case node_tag of
        0 -> return Empty
        1 -> do
            node_value <- get
            left_subtree <- get-tree-v1
            right_subtree <- get-tree-v1
            node_height <- fromIntegral <$> getWord32be
            return $ Node node_value left_subtree right_subtree node_height Nothing
        _ -> fail "Invalid tree node tag"

-- Convenience functions for serialization
serialize :: Binary a => BinaryTree a -> BS.ByteString
serialize = encode

deserialize :: Binary a => BS.ByteString -> BinaryTree a
deserialize = decode

                q1 = float(cp.percentile(arr, 25))

                iqr = q3 - q1
                lower_bound = q1 - threshold * iqr

                mask = (arr < lower_bound) | (arr > upper_bound)
                outliers = pd.Series(cp.asnumpy(mask), index=data.index)
            else:

            else:
                q1 = np.percentile(arr, 25)
                q3 = np.percentile(arr, 75)
                iqr = q3 - q1
                lower_bound = q1 - threshold * iqr
                upper_bound = q3 + threshold * iqr
                outliers = pd.Series((data < lower_bound) | (data > upper_bound), index=data.index)
                
        elif method.lower() == 'zscore':
            if self.use_gpu:
                mean = cp.mean(arr)
                std = cp.std(arr)
                z_scores = cp.abs((arr - mean) / std)
                outliers = pd.Series(cp.asnumpy(z_scores > threshold), index=data.index)
            else:
                z_scores = np.abs(stats.zscore(arr))
                outliers = pd.Series(z_scores > threshold, index=data.index)
                
        elif method.lower() == 'mad':
            if self.use_gpu:
                median = cp.median(arr)
                mad = cp.median(cp.abs(arr - median))
                outliers = pd.Series(cp.asnumpy(cp.abs(arr - median) / mad > threshold), index=data.index)
            else:
                median = np.median(arr)
                mad = np.median(np.abs(arr - median))
                outliers = pd.Series(np.abs(arr - median) / mad > threshold, index=data.index)
        else:
            raise ValueError("Method must be one of 'iqr', 'zscore', or 'mad'")
        
        self.outliers = outliers
        
        if return_indices:
            return data[outliers]
        else:
            result = pd.DataFrame({'value': data, 'is_outlier': outliers})
            return result

        axes[plot_idx].plot(self.time_series, label='Original Data')

            else:

        # Seasonality components
        if include_components and self.seasonality_components is not None:

            # Trend component

                'trend': components['trend'].describe().to_dict(),
                'seasonal': components['seasonal'].describe().to_dict(),

            axes[plot_idx].scatter(outlier_indices, outlier_values, color='red', label='Outliers')
            axes[plot_idx].set_title('Outlier Detection')
            axes[plot_idx].legend()

            report['outliers'] = {

            report['saved_to'] = output_path
            
        report['figure'] = fig

-- Types
data MerkleTree = Leaf BS.ByteString
                | Node MerkleTree MerkleTree
                deriving (Show, Eq)

  { sender :: BS.ByteString
  , recipient :: BS.ByteString
  , amount :: Double

  } deriving (Show, Eq)

data BlockHeader = BlockHeader
  { prevBlockHash :: BS.ByteString

  , timestamp :: Integer

  } deriving (Show, Eq)

-- Merkle tree validation
validateMerkleTree :: MerkleTree -> [Transaction] -> Bool
validateMerkleTree tree transactions = 
  let computed-tree = buildMerkleTree transactions
  in computed-tree == tree

buildMerkleTree :: [Transaction] -> MerkleTree
buildMerkleTree [] = Leaf (SHA256.hash BS.empty)
buildMerkleTree [tx] = Leaf (hashTransaction tx)
buildMerkleTree txs =
  let left-txs = fst split-txs
      right-txs = snd split-txs
      split-txs = splitAt (length txs `div` 2) txs
      left-tree = buildMerkleTree left-txs
      right-tree = buildMerkleTree right-txs
  in Node left-tree right-tree

hashTransaction :: Transaction -> BS.ByteString
hashTransaction tx = SHA256.hash (BS.concat 
  [ sender tx
  , recipient tx
  , BS.pack (show (amount tx))
  , BS.pack (show (fee tx))
  , signature tx
  , BS.pack (show (nonce tx))
  ])

verifyProofOfWork :: Block -> Integer -> Bool
verifyProofOfWork block target-difficulty =
  let header = blockHeader block
      block-hash = hashBlockHeader header
      leading-zeros = countLeadingZeros block-hash
  in leading-zeros >= difficulty header && difficulty header >= target-difficulty

hashBlockHeader :: BlockHeader -> BS.ByteString
hashBlockHeader header = SHA256.hash (BS.concat
  [ prevBlockHash header
  , merkleRoot header
  , BS.pack (show (timestamp header))
  , BS.pack (show (difficulty header))

  ])

countLeadingZeros :: BS.ByteString -> Integer
countLeadingZeros bs =

module BlockchainValidator

  , verifyDigitalSignature
  , calculateTransactionFee

import qualified Data.ByteString as BS
import qualified Data.Map as Map
import qualified Crypto.Hash.SHA256 as SHA256
import qualified Data.Binary as Binary
import qualified Network.Socket as Socket
import qualified Control.Concurrent as Concurrent

-- Types
data MerkleTree = Leaf BS.ByteString
                | Node MerkleTree MerkleTree
                deriving (Show, Eq)

data Transaction = Transaction
  { sender :: BS.ByteString
  , recipient :: BS.ByteString
  , amount :: Double
  , fee :: Double
  , signature :: BS.ByteString
  , nonce :: Integer
  } deriving (Show, Eq)

data Block = Block
  { blockHeader :: BlockHeader
  , transactions :: [Transaction]
  } deriving (Show, Eq)

data BlockHeader = BlockHeader
  { prevBlockHash :: BS.ByteString
  , merkleRoot :: BS.ByteString
  , timestamp :: Integer
  , difficulty :: Integer
  , nonce :: Integer
  } deriving (Show, Eq)

data SmartContract = SmartContract
  { contractCode :: BS.ByteString
  , contractState :: Map.Map String BS.ByteString
  , contractOwner :: BS.ByteString
  } deriving (Show, Eq)

-- Merkle tree validation
validateMerkleTree :: MerkleTree -> [Transaction] -> Bool
validateMerkleTree tree transactions = 
  let computed-tree = buildMerkleTree transactions
  in computed-tree == tree

buildMerkleTree :: [Transaction] -> MerkleTree
buildMerkleTree [] = Leaf (SHA256.hash BS.empty)
buildMerkleTree [tx] = Leaf (hashTransaction tx)
buildMerkleTree txs =
  let left-txs = fst split-txs
      right-txs = snd split-txs
      split-txs = splitAt (length txs `div` 2) txs
      left-tree = buildMerkleTree left-txs
      right-tree = buildMerkleTree right-txs
  in Node left-tree right-tree

hashTransaction :: Transaction -> BS.ByteString
hashTransaction tx = SHA256.hash (BS.concat 
  [ sender tx
  , recipient tx
  , BS.pack (show (amount tx))
  , BS.pack (show (fee tx))
  , signature tx
  , BS.pack (show (nonce tx))
  ])

-- Proof-of-work consensus
verifyProofOfWork :: Block -> Integer -> Bool
verifyProofOfWork block target-difficulty =
  let header = blockHeader block
      block-hash = hashBlockHeader header
      leading-zeros = countLeadingZeros block-hash
  in leading-zeros >= difficulty header && difficulty header >= target-difficulty

hashBlockHeader :: BlockHeader -> BS.ByteString
hashBlockHeader header = SHA256.hash (BS.concat
  [ prevBlockHash header
  , merkleRoot header
  , BS.pack (show (timestamp header))
  , BS.pack (show (difficulty header))
  , BS.pack (show (nonce header))
  ])

countLeadingZeros :: BS.ByteString -> Integer
countLeadingZeros bs = 
  let bits = concatMap (\byte -> map (testBit byte) [7,6..0]) (BS.unpack bs)
      leading-zeros = length (takeWhile (== False) bits)
  in fromIntegral leading-zeros

-- Smart contract execution
executeSmartContract :: SmartContract -> Transaction -> IO (Either String SmartContract)
executeSmartContract contract tx = do
  -- This is a simplified implementation
  -- In a real system, this would involve running contract code in a VM
  if verifyContractExecution contract tx
    then do
      let new-state = updateContractState contract tx
      return $ Right $ contract { contractState = new-state }
    else
      return $ Left "Smart contract execution failed"

verifyContractExecution :: SmartContract -> Transaction -> Bool
verifyContractExecution contract tx =
  -- Simplified contract verification logic
  sender tx /= contractOwner contract || amount tx > 0

updateContractState :: SmartContract -> Transaction -> Map.Map String BS.ByteString
updateContractState contract tx =
  -- Simplified state update
  Map.insert (show (nonce tx)) (BS.concat [sender tx, recipient tx]) (contractState contract)

-- Digital signature verification
verifyDigitalSignature :: Transaction -> BS.ByteString -> Bool
verifyDigitalSignature tx public-key =
  -- In a real implementation, this would use a cryptographic library
  -- to verify the signature against the transaction data with the public key
  let tx-data = BS.concat
        [ sender tx
        , recipient tx
        , BS.pack (show (amount tx))
        , BS.pack (show (fee tx))
        , BS.pack (show (nonce tx))
        ]
      expected-sig = SHA256.hash (BS.append tx-data public-key)
  in signature tx == expected-sig

-- Transaction fee calculation
calculateTransactionFee :: Transaction -> Double -> Double
calculateTransactionFee tx base-fee =
  let tx-size = fromIntegral $ BS.length (sender tx) + BS.length (recipient tx) +
                16 + -- 8 bytes each for amount and fee
                BS.length (signature tx) +
                8   -- 8 bytes for nonce
      size-multiplier = tx-size / 1024.0  -- per KB
      priority-fee = fee tx
  in base-fee * size-multiplier + priority-fee

-- Network synchronization
synchronizeNetwork :: Socket.Socket -> [Block] -> IO (Either String [Block])
synchronizeNetwork socket local-blockchain = do
  -- Request latest blockchain from network
  remote-blockchain-result <- requestRemoteBlockchain socket
  case remote-blockchain-result of
    Left err -> return $ Left err
    Right remote-blockchain -> do
      -- Compare and resolve conflicts
      if isValidBlockchain remote-blockchain && 
         (blockchainLength remote-blockchain > blockchainLength local-blockchain)
        then return $ Right remote-blockchain
        else return $ Right local-blockchain

requestRemoteBlockchain :: Socket.Socket -> IO (Either String [Block])

isValidBlockchain [_] = True
isValidBlockchain (b1:b2:bs) =
  prevBlockHash (blockHeader b2) == hashBlockHeader (blockHeader b1) &&
  isValidBlockchain (b2:bs)

blockchainLength :: [Block] -> Integer
blockchainLength = fromIntegral . length

The class should support multiple data sources such as CSV, JSON, and databases. Implement methods extract_data(), transform_data(), and load_data() to manage each ETL stage. Ensure efficient memory usage and concurrency handling. Follow the naming conventions where class names are in PascalCase and function names are in snake_case.

import pandas as pd
import json
import sqlite3
import concurrent.futures
from typing import Dict, List, Any, Union, Optional
import logging
import threading

class DataPipeline:
    """
    A class to handle ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) operations for real-time analytics.
    Supports multiple data sources and efficient memory/concurrency handling.
    """
    
    def __init__(self, config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None):
        self.config = config or {}
        self.data = None
        self.transformed_data = None
        self._lock = threading.Lock()
        self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
        
    def extract_data(self, source: str, source_path: str, **kwargs) -> pd.DataFrame:
        """
        Extract data from various sources (CSV, JSON, database)
        
        Args:
            source: Type of data source ('csv', 'json', 'database')
            source_path: Path to the file or connection string
            **kwargs: Additional parameters for extraction
            
        Returns:
            DataFrame containing the extracted data
        """
        self.logger.info(f"Extracting data from {source}: {source_path}")
        
        if source.lower() == 'csv':
            self.data = pd.read_csv(source_path, **kwargs)
        elif source.lower() == 'json':
            with open(source_path, 'r') as f:
                json_data = json.load(f)
            self.data = pd.DataFrame(json_data)
        elif source.lower() == 'database':
            query = kwargs.get('query', None)
            if not query:
                raise ValueError("SQL query must be provided for database extraction")
            
            conn = sqlite3.connect(source_path)
            self.data = pd.read_sql_query(query, conn)
            conn.close()
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported data source: {source}")
        
        return self.data
    
    def transform_data(self, transformations: List[Dict[str, Any]], batch_size: int = 1000) -> pd.DataFrame:
        """
        Apply transformations to the extracted data in batches
        
        Args:
            transformations: List of transformation operations to apply
            batch_size: Size of batches for processing
            
        Returns:
            DataFrame containing the transformed data
        """
        if self.data is None:
            raise ValueError("No data available. Please extract data first.")
            
        self.logger.info(f"Transforming data with {len(transformations)} operations")
        
        # Process in batches to manage memory
        num_batches = len(self.data) // batch_size + (1 if len(self.data) % batch_size > 0 else 0)
        transformed_batches = []
        
        for i in range(num_batches):
            start_idx = i * batch_size
            end_idx = min((i + 1) * batch_size, len(self.data))
            batch = self.data.iloc[start_idx:end_idx].copy()
            
            # Apply each transformation to the batch
            for transform in transformations:
                operation = transform.get('operation')
                params = transform.get('params', {})
                
                if operation == 'filter':
                    column = params.get('column')
                    condition = params.get('condition')
                    value = params.get('value')
                    
                    if condition == 'equals':
                        batch = batch[batch[column] == value]
                    elif condition == 'greater_than':
                        batch = batch[batch[column] > value]
                    elif condition == 'less_than':
                        batch = batch[batch[column] < value]
                    
                elif operation == 'add_column':
                    column = params.get('column')
                    expression = params.get('expression')
                    batch[column] = batch.eval(expression)
                    
                elif operation == 'drop_columns':
                    columns = params.get('columns', [])
                    batch = batch.drop(columns=columns)
                    
                elif operation == 'rename_columns':
                    mapping = params.get('mapping', {})
                    batch = batch.rename(columns=mapping)
                    
                elif operation == 'aggregate':
                    group_by = params.get('group_by', [])
                    agg_funcs = params.get('agg_funcs', {})
                    batch = batch.groupby(group_by).agg(agg_funcs).reset_index()
            
            transformed_batches.append(batch)
        
        with self._lock:
            self.transformed_data = pd.concat(transformed_batches, ignore_index=True)
            
        return self.transformed_data
    
    def load_data(self, destination: str, destination_path: str, **kwargs) -> bool:
        """
        Load transformed data to various destinations
        
        Args:
            destination: Type of destination ('csv', 'json', 'database')
            destination_path: Path to save the file or connection string
            **kwargs: Additional parameters for loading
            
        Returns:
            Boolean indicating success
        """
        if self.transformed_data is None:
            raise ValueError("No transformed data available. Please transform data first.")
            
        self.logger.info(f"Loading data to {destination}: {destination_path}")
        
        try:
            if destination.lower() == 'csv':
                self.transformed_data.to_csv(destination_path, index=False, **kwargs)
            elif destination.lower() == 'json':
                orient = kwargs.get('orient', 'records')
                self.transformed_data.to_json(destination_path, orient=orient, **kwargs)
            elif destination.lower() == 'database':
                table_name = kwargs.get('table_name', 'transformed_data')
                if_exists = kwargs.get('if_exists', 'replace')
                
                conn = sqlite3.connect(destination_path)
                self.transformed_data.to_sql(table_name, conn, if_exists=if_exists, index=False)
                conn.close()
            else:
                raise ValueError(f"Unsupported destination: {destination}")
                
            return True
        
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Error loading data: {str(e)}")
            return False
            
    def run_parallel_extraction(self, sources: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[pd.DataFrame]:
        """
        Extract data from multiple sources in parallel
        
        Args:
            sources: List of source configurations
            
        Returns:
            List of DataFrames from each source
        """
        results = []
        
        with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor() as executor:
            futures = []
            for source_config in sources:
                source_type = source_config.get('type')
                source_path = source_config.get('path')
                kwargs = source_config.get('params', {})
                
                future = executor.submit(
                    self.extract_data,
                    source_type,
                    source_path,
                    **kwargs
                )
                futures.append(future)
            
            for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures):
                results.append(future.result())
        
        return results
